name: find_in_files
description: Search multiple patterns in files with filtering, find files by name glob or regex, or list directory contents.

parameters.name: --name-regex=PATTERN
parameters.description: Filter file paths by extended regex (replaces find | grep -E pipelines); works with both --names-only and content search

parameters.name: --ls
parameters.description: List directory contents (like ls -la) for one or more directories

parameters.name: --path=PATTERN
parameters.description: Include only files/dirs whose path matches find -path pattern; works in both content search and --names-only modes (repeatable)

parameters.name: --max-results=N
parameters.description: Max output lines (default: 50 for content, 100 for names-only)

parameters.name: -t
parameters.description: File type: f (file) or d (dir) for --names-only

parameters.name: --no-exclude-defaults
parameters.description: Don't auto-exclude .git, node_modules, venv, and __pycache__ paths (--names-only mode)

examples.expected: Files under calypso-url directories matching .ts or .tsx extensions

cost_saving_rationale: Collapses multi-pattern grep -rn calls, find | xargs grep pipelines, and ls -la into single steps; --name-regex=PATTERN replaces find | grep -E regex path filtering (seen in hotspot samples) eliminating the grep pipeline step; --path=PATTERN enables native find -path filtering in both content search and --names-only modes, replacing multi-step find -path + grep pipelines seen in samples; default --max-results eliminates head -N piping.
